title: Ordering, Single Active Consumer, Keys, and Partitioning · Contract Lens: Keyed Causal Order
summary: A service promises global order across publishers and channels, adds parallel consumers, requeues failures, uses priority, then depends on exact FIFO completion for each customer. Planned artifact: Add and verify the keyed causal order increment in ordering scope and partition strategy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Partition related messages by stable business key while bounding skew, hot keys, resharding, and cross-key independence.
State ordering scope across producer channel, exchange route, one queue, delivery, completion, redelivery, priority, and multiple consumers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Keyed Causal Order · Theory Keyed Causal Order
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 9930 in a high-volume telemetry route with one hot customer key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce partition, version, gap, and single-active evidence instead of claiming global order; separate browser evidence from disposable live-broker proof; then add one subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Partition related messages by stable business key while bounding skew, hot keys, resharding, and cross-key independence.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Keyed Causal Order contract for the supplied data.
